findings.title: Implement One-Click Purchase Feature
findings.description: Develop a one-click purchase flow for returning customers that utilizes saved payment methods to streamline the checkout process.
assumptions: Users are familiar with one-click purchasing.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a one-click purchase flow for returning customers with saved payment methods.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of the one-click purchase feature.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a returning customer with saved payment methods WHEN they initiate checkout THEN they should see a one-click purchase option that completes the transaction without additional steps.

findings.title: Address Checkout API Latency
findings.description: Optimize the checkout API to reduce p95 latency from 1200ms to below 500ms to meet service level objectives.
assumptions: Current API latency is affecting user experience.
Optimizations can be made without major architectural changes.

evidence.excerpt: Current checkout API p95 latency is 1,200ms which exceeds our 500ms SLO.

recommendations: Implement caching and optimize the payment validation pipeline.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the checkout API WHEN measured THEN the p95 latency should be less than 500ms.

findings.title: Fix Payment Retry Reliability
findings.description: Resolve issues with payment retries that are failing silently due to idempotency key collisions and race conditions.
assumptions: Payment retries can be made reliable with code changes.
Users expect their payments to be processed correctly.

evidence.excerpt: Payment retries are failing silently in 3.8% of cases due to idempotency key collisions.

recommendations: Implement robust error handling for payment retries.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a payment retry scenario WHEN a payment fails THEN the retry should succeed without silent failures.

findings.title: Implement Auto-Apply Best Coupon Feature
findings.description: Automatically detect and apply the best available coupon at checkout to reduce friction and improve conversion rates.
assumptions: Users prefer not to manually enter coupon codes.
The system can accurately determine the best coupon.

evidence.excerpt: Automatically detect and apply the best available coupon or discount code at checkout.

recommendations: Develop the auto-apply coupon feature as part of the checkout flow.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user at checkout WHEN they have eligible coupons THEN the best coupon should be automatically applied to their order.

findings.title: Improve Checkout Reliability on Mobile
findings.description: Fix the checkout crashes reported on mobile devices when switching payment methods to ensure a stable experience.
assumptions: The crashes can be reproduced and fixed.
Users expect a seamless experience on mobile.

evidence.excerpt: Checkout page crashes on iOS Safari and Android Chrome when switching between payment methods.

recommendations: Conduct thorough testing on mobile devices to identify and fix crashes.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user on mobile WHEN switching payment methods THEN the checkout should not crash.

findings.title: Migrate to New Payment Gateway
findings.description: Migrate the payment processing to StripeConnect to reduce transaction fees and improve international support.
assumptions: Migration can be completed without major disruptions.

evidence.excerpt: Migration to StripeConnect is planned to reduce transaction fees by 0.3%.

recommendations: Plan and execute the migration to StripeConnect.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the migration is complete WHEN processing payments THEN transaction fees should be reduced by 0.3%.

findings.title: Monitor Checkout Conversion Rate
findings.description: Establish monitoring for the checkout conversion rate to track improvements post-implementation of new features.
assumptions: Monitoring tools can be set up to track conversion rates effectively.

evidence.excerpt: The primary measure of success for the QuickPay Checkout Redesign.

recommendations: Implement monitoring tools to track the checkout conversion rate.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring is in place WHEN users complete purchases THEN the checkout conversion rate should be tracked and reported.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback on Checkout Experience
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to collect user feedback on the checkout experience to identify pain points and areas for improvement.
assumptions: Users are willing to provide feedback after checkout.

evidence.excerpt: Users express a strong desire for a one-click purchase option.

recommendations: Create a feedback form post-checkout to gather user insights.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is in place WHEN users complete a purchase THEN they should be prompted to provide feedback.

findings.title: Enhance Transparency in Coupon Application
findings.description: Provide users with visibility into coupon application processes to address concerns over auto-applying discounts.
assumptions: Users prefer to see all available discounts.

evidence.excerpt: Some users are skeptical about auto-applying coupons.

recommendations: Display all available coupons and the applied coupon during checkout.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is at checkout WHEN coupons are available THEN all options should be displayed clearly.

findings.title: Conduct User Journey Analytics
findings.description: Implement detailed user journey analytics to capture user behavior throughout the checkout process.
assumptions: User journey analytics can provide insights into friction points.

evidence.excerpt: Current metrics do not capture detailed user journey analytics.

recommendations: Set up analytics tools to track user journeys during checkout.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the analytics tools are in place WHEN users navigate the checkout THEN their journey should be tracked and reported.

findings.title: Implement User Segmentation for B2B Buyers
findings.description: Create a tailored checkout experience for B2B buyers that prioritizes reliability and efficiency.
assumptions: B2B buyers have different needs compared to regular consumers.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise buyers emphasize the need for a reliable checkout process.

recommendations: Develop features that cater specifically to B2B buyer needs.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a B2B buyer is at checkout WHEN they complete their purchase THEN the process should prioritize reliability and efficiency.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics has led to the creation of a structured backlog for the QuickPay Checkout Redesign. Key requirements focus on enhancing the checkout experience through features like one-click purchasing, improving reliability, and addressing user concerns over coupon applications. The backlog includes a mix of critical, high, and medium priority items, ensuring that both immediate needs and future enhancements are considered.
